text: The da Vinci robotic surgical system is shown with four robotic arms: one arm holds two endoscopic cameras, while the other three arms are used to perform delicate surgical procedures.

The surgeon operates the da Vinci robotic system from an ergonomically designed terminal, viewing the surgical field in three-dimensional high definition through two offset cameras. The surgeon uses hand controllers to direct the robot's movements, while foot pedals provide additional control for tasks like cauterization. The robotic arms mimic human wrist rotation, offering a full range of movement.
